full_response:
The vector \(v^a\) has components \(v^0 = 5\), \(v^1 = -1\), \(v^2 = 0\), and \(v^3 = 1\) in frame R. The vector \(-6v^a\) is obtained by multiplying each component of \(v^a\) by \(-6\).

\[
\begin{aligned}
(-6v^0) &= -6 \times 5 = -30, \\
(-6v^1) &= -6 \times (-1) = 6, \\
(-6v^2) &= -6 \times 0 = 0, \\
(-6v^3) &= -6 \times 1 = -6.
\end{aligned}
\]

Thus, the components of \(-6v^a\) in frame R are \((-30, 6, 0, -6)\).

\boxed{(-30, 6, 0, -6)}
Confidence: 100%
